Confirm the framework, routing model, styling stack, animation libraries, current page architecture, and whether framer-motion or motion/react is installed. Confirm whether any Aceternity UI components already exist; do not install anything. Check that there is no app/[...slug]/page.tsx and no components/PublicRoutePage.tsx. Search for PublicRoutePage imports and slug catch-all references. Check whether the landing page has all required homepage sections: Hero, Enterprise Quick Search, Garage Foundry Factory journey, What We Build, Interactive Experience, Global Presence, Client Success, Live Dashboard, Latest Research, Contact, Footer. Check whether all public pages are explicit App Router pages. Check whether the Blueprint scoring formula exists and whether the banking test case can return 87/100. Produce a gap list only. Do not edit files. Final response must include files inspected, missing items, route gaps, homepage gaps, design-system observations, animation-library availability, and recommended next task.

title: Verify and fix Blueprint Generator scoring
prompt: Verify and fix the Blueprint Generator. It must be frontend-only and may appear on homepage and /build/blueprint. It must include title: Get Your Enterprise AI Blueprint in 60 Seconds. Required inputs: Industry, Company Size, Top Priorities with max 3 selection, AI Journey, Data Readiness, Leadership Commitment, Biggest Challenge, Email, OTP placeholder. Optional: Budget and Geography. It must not send real OTP or call backend. Formula: Blueprint Score = 0.20(AI Maturity)+0.25(Business Need)+0.20(Data Readiness)+0.20(Process Complexity)+0.15(Transformation Readiness). Scoring: No AI 10, Exploring AI 30, Running Pilots 60, Scaling AI 85, AI-Driven Enterprise or AI-Native 100. Business Need: Cost Reduction 20, Productivity 20, Customer Experience 15, Revenue Growth 15, Compliance 10, AI Transformation 20, Automate Processes 20, capped at 100. Data Readiness: Highly fragmented 20, Partially connected 50, Mostly integrated 75, Fully integrated 100. Process Complexity: <100 20, 100-1000 50, 1000-10000 80, 10000+ 100. Transformation Readiness: Not Discussed 20, Exploring 50, Budget Approved 80, Executive Mandate 100. Categories: 0-25 AI Beginner, 26-50 AI Explorer, 51-70 AI Adopter, 71-85 AI Transformer, 86-100 AI-Native Leader. Test example Banking, 15000 employees, Scaling AI, Productivity + Cost Reduction + Compliance, Mostly Integrated Data, Budget Approved must display 87/100. Required outputs: AI Readiness Score, Top 5 Recommended AI Opportunities, Recommended GFF AI Solution, Expected Business Impact, 90-Day Roadmap. Label impacts as expected/modelled, not guaranteed. Run lint/build.

Required client portal routes: /portal/dashboard, /portal/projects, /portal/projects/[id] if the current project supports dynamic explicit folders safely, /portal/ai-operations, /portal/ai-operations/[id] if safe, /portal/documents, /portal/documents/[id] if safe, /portal/billing, /portal/billing/invoices, /portal/billing/invoices/[id] if safe, /portal/support, /portal/support/[id] if safe, /portal/analytics, /portal/governance, /portal/settings, /portal/activity. Required compatibility client routes: /client, /client/control-center, /client/dashboard if useful. Required admin routes: /admin, /admin/dashboard, /admin/clients, /admin/clients/[id] if safe, /admin/projects, /admin/projects/[id] if safe, /admin/users, /admin/ai-operations, /admin/documents, /admin/billing, /admin/support, /admin/analytics, /admin/governance, /admin/settings, /admin/activity. If dynamic routes are risky in the current automation, create explicit list/detail pages without dynamic params and use local state/detail drawers instead. Pages may initially render shared shell placeholders, but every route must exist explicitly. Run lint and build.

title: Build local auth session and role-based preview layer
prompt: Build a frontend-only local auth/session preview layer for Phase 4. Do not add real authentication. Do not call APIs. Do not add backend, cookies requiring server auth, JWT verification, OAuth, Cognito, Firebase, Clerk, Supabase, or database. Implement a safe local preview system using localStorage or in-memory state with hydration guards. Support roles: client_admin, client_member, gff_admin, gff_operator, finance_admin, support_agent, viewer. Provide local preview users with clearly fake/internal preview labels, not real identities. Add helper utilities such as getPreviewSession, setPreviewSession, clearPreviewSession, hasRole, canAccessPortalSection, canAccessAdminSection. Add safe route guard components that redirect or show a locked preview state when no local preview session exists. Every message must say this is frontend preview only and real auth will be connected in backend phase. Do not make it look like real security. Run lint and build.

title: Build Client Login public landing at /portal
prompt: Upgrade /portal into a premium Client Login landing page. This page is public and should explain the secure delivery workspace before login. Do not touch the main landing page. /portal must contain module previews: Dashboard, Projects, Invoices or Billing, Support, Documents, AI Operations, Analytics, Governance. It should have a premium login CTA, secure workspace narrative, module preview cards, trust/security style visual language without fake certifications, and a clear note that login/auth is frontend preview only in this phase. Add route links to /portal/login and preview module anchors or cards. Design should feel expensive, secure, enterprise-grade, and consistent with the GFF AI theme. Use polished graphics, bento cards, dashboard previews without fake data, and subtle motion. No real auth, no API calls. Run lint and build.

title: Build premium login page and local role preview
prompt: Build premium frontend-only login pages for /login and /portal/login. Do not touch landing page. The login UI should feel like a secure enterprise product login, with GFF AI branding, email field, password field placeholder, SSO button placeholders, OTP login placeholder, role preview selector for local development, and clear frontend-only notice. On submit, create a local preview session only and route client roles to /portal/dashboard and GFF/admin roles to /admin/dashboard. Do not implement real auth, password validation beyond simple required fields, OTP sending, SSO, OAuth, sessions, cookies, JWT, backend calls, or API calls. Add forgot password placeholder link and contact support link. Make it mobile excellent, accessible, keyboard-friendly, and visually premium with subtle motion. Run lint and build.

title: Build forgot password and OTP preview states
prompt: Add frontend-only forgot password and OTP preview states to the login experience. Do not create real password reset, email, OTP, or backend calls. The forgot password UI should allow entering an email and then show a safe preview message: password reset will be connected in the backend authentication phase. The OTP UI should allow entering a 6 digit placeholder code only for preview and must not pretend to send or verify real OTP. Add accessible validation, clear microcopy, and safe reset/back actions. The UI should be polished and secure-looking but honest. Do not touch landing page. Run lint and build.

title: Build private workspace app shell
target: shared client and admin app shell
prompt: Build the shared private app shell used by client portal and admin portal pages. Do not touch landing page. The sidebar should support grouped navigation and collapse behavior on desktop, bottom or drawer navigation on mobile, and clear active states. Client portal navigation must include Dashboard, Projects, AI Operations, Documents, Billing, Support, Analytics, Governance, Activity, Settings. Admin navigation must include Dashboard, Clients, Projects, Users, AI Operations, Documents, Billing, Support, Analytics, Governance, Activity, Settings. Make the UI feel like a premium enterprise control center, not a basic admin template. Use current theme, subtle motion, accessible landmarks, keyboard focus, and mobile-first behavior. Run lint and build.

title: Create local mock data model for portal and admin

Include types for ClientAccount, Project, Program, AgentOperation, AgentHealth, DocumentItem, Invoice, SupportTicket, AnalyticsMetric, GovernanceItem, Approval, User, Role, ActivityEvent, Notification. Include deterministic sample entries such as representative client account, representative project names, representative agent names, preview invoices, preview tickets. No fake logos or real client names. Centralize in a safe local data file. Run lint and build.

Use these components for Projects, Documents, Billing, Support, Users, Clients, AI Operations, Governance, and Activity. Make the design feel custom and expensive, not default HTML tables. Use subtle row hover, sticky header where useful, and clean responsive cards on mobile. No backend calls. Run lint and build.

Use motion lightly. Run lint and build.

Add CTA to request update or contact support using frontend-only action. No backend. Mobile card view required. Run lint and build.
